name: Database Baseline
description: Guards irreversible database operations: schema drops, privilege escalation, truncation, unqualified deletes, and direct prod database access. Requires admin approval via access request for DDL destruction and prod connections; requires justification for DML-without-WHERE.

rules.name: Require access request for DROP DATABASE, TABLE, or SCHEMA
rules.description: DROP DATABASE, DROP TABLE, and DROP SCHEMA permanently destroy data structures and their contents. This matches across psql, mysql, sqlite, and direct CLI usage. Requires admin approval before proceeding.

rules.name: Require access request for GRANT ALL privileges
rules.description: GRANT ALL on a table or database gives full access to a user or role, which can enable privilege escalation or unintended data exposure. Requires admin approval before proceeding.

rules.name: Require access request for DROP USER
rules.description: Dropping a database user removes their access and may break service accounts or applications that depend on that user. Requires admin approval before proceeding.

rules.name: Require access request to connect to a prod database
rules.description: Connecting directly to a prod-named database (via psql, mysql, or mongo) allows live data manipulation. Requires admin approval.

rules.name: Require justification for TRUNCATE TABLE
rules.description: TRUNCATE removes all rows from a table instantly, with no WHERE clause and limited rollback options in many engines. Requires a written reason.

rules.name: Require justification for DELETE FROM statements
rules.description: Any DELETE FROM statement requires a written justification. This catches both targeted deletes (with WHERE) and full-table deletes (without WHERE), ensuring intent is documented.
